hyperedges.label: Layered Agent Architecture
pnpm Workspace Package Set
Planned Refactor: New Extracted Classes
Supported Minecraft Versions in Prismarine Viewer
Texture Data File Types Used Per Version
1.16.4 Full Texture Dataset (first version with all 5 file types)
1.17.1 Full Texture Dataset (all 5 file types)
1.13.2 Partial Texture Dataset (3 file types, no models/states)
All agent tools implement ToolResult contract
Coordinator orchestrates BotHandle lifecycle via createBot
Test suite covering all agent tools
A* pathfinding core components
Goal class hierarchy
Entity rendering pipeline
All Tool execute() Functions sharing ToolResult contract
Chunk meshing pipeline: WorldRenderer -> Worker -> World -> getSectionGeometry
Viewer render loop: Viewer + WorldRenderer + Entities + Primitives + THREE.js scene
Mineflayer integration: mineflayer plugin + WorldView + socket.io bridging bot state to browser
Block rendering: getSectionGeometry + renderElement + biome tint + AO
Core pathfinding pipeline: AStar + Movements + Goals + Move
Goal class hierarchy (all concrete goal implementations)
Movement execution system: physics, locking, smoothing
Core rendering pipeline: Viewer + WorldRenderer + WorldView + Workers
World export examples (screenshot + 3D model)
Dev Server orchestrates WorldEditor, ScenarioManager, MarkerManager, TestRunner, PathVisualizer
WorldLayout flows through ChunkRelay, WorldEditor, ScenarioManager, and setup()
App.svelte composes TestPanel, ViewerPanel, WorldEditor Svelte panels
pnpm workspace inter-package dependency graph: agent → pathfinder, agent → test-suite (dev), test-suite → prismarine-viewer, prismarine-viewer → pathfinder (dev)
dev-server domain objects: ScenarioManager, WorldEditor, MarkerManager, TestRunner, PrismarinePathVisualizer all instantiated inside startDevServer
Agent tool execute pattern: StartCombat and Chat both export execute(bot, args) → Promise<ToolResult>

nodes.label: { pathfinder, Movements, goals: { GoalNear, GoalCompositeAny, GoalCompositeAll } }
nodes.norm_label: { pathfinder, movements, goals: { goalnear, goalcompositeany, goalcompositeall } }

nodes.label: LLM Strategic Layer
nodes.rationale: Runs asynchronously every ~2s to avoid blocking the tick loop. Intentional design: the server must never wait for LLM responses to preserve real-time agent behavior validity in research findings.

nodes.label: RL Combat Layer
nodes.rationale: Uses PPO (OpenPVP, BridgeFight, BedDefense) — standard choice for discrete action spaces; goal is functional combat, not state-of-the-art RL. BridgeFight and BedDefense use LSTM for temporal patterns.

nodes.label: Reactive Interrupt Layer
nodes.rationale: Hardcoded survival behaviors that always run every tick at highest priority regardless of other layers. Not optional or deferrable.

nodes.label: Natural Language Agent Communication
nodes.rationale: Agents communicate via Minecraft chat in natural language rather than structured schemas. Intentional research decision: the research question concerns what agents choose to say and how that evolves — structured vocab would answer a different question. The messiness is the point.

nodes.label: Research Question: Emergent Behavior Under Competitive Pressure
nodes.norm_label: research question: emergent behavior under competitive pressure

nodes.label: rl-env (Python)
nodes.rationale: Separate from rl-pvp so the physics simulation can remain stable while training is iterated on. No Mineflayer dependency — runs headless for fast parallel training.

nodes.label: bedwars-server (Java Paper plugin)
nodes.rationale: Fork of Bedwars plugin that adds local HTTP/WebSocket API for research infrastructure. Chosen over Fabric mod because Paper was already in use and the API is sufficient for observation/control needs. Plugin never touches agent behavior — purely observational.

nodes.label: Refactor Plan: Consolidate Dev Server + Viewer Architecture
nodes.norm_label: refactor plan: consolidate dev server + viewer architecture

nodes.label: ChunkRelay.ts (planned)
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for layout application + chunk resend. Eliminates duplication between setup.ts and dev-server.ts.

nodes.label: Project Coding Standards
nodes.rationale: SRP, no spaghetti code, self-documenting names, comment only the why, remove dead code, workspace:* protocol for cross-package deps, submodule-first commit workflow.
